examples.title: FTP File Download Example
examples.description: Demonstrates how to connect to FTP server and download a file using Python's ftplib
examples.code:
import ftplib

# Connect to FTP server
ftp = ftplib.FTP('ftp.example.com')
ftp.login('username', 'password')

# Change directory
ftp.cwd('/public/files')

# Download file
with open('downloaded.txt', 'wb') as f:
    ftp.retrbinary('RETR sample.txt', f.write)

# List files
print(ftp.nlst())

# Quit FTP session
ftp.quit()
examples.explanation: This code shows basic FTP operations: connect using FTP(), login with credentials, change directory with cwd(), download file using retrbinary(), and properly quit with quit().

examples.title: HTTP GET Request Example
examples.description: Shows how to send HTTP GET request and process response using Python requests library
examples.code:
import requests

# Send GET request
response = requests.get('https://api.example.com/data')

# Check status code
if response.status_code == 200:
    # Parse JSON response
    data = response.json()
    print(data)
else:
    print(f'Error: {response.status_code}')

# Display headers
print(response.headers)

# POST request example
payload = {'username': 'user', 'password': 'pass'}
post_response = requests.post('https://api.example.com/login', json=payload)
examples.explanation: Demonstrates HTTP GET for fetching data and POST for sending data. Uses requests library for simplicity. Status codes indicate success (200) or errors.

examples.title: SMTP Email Sending Example
examples.description: Demonstrates sending email using Python's smtplib with SMTP protocol
examples.code:
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText

# Email configuration
sender = 'sender@example.com'
receiver = 'receiver@example.com'
subject = 'Test Email'
body = 'This is a test email sent via SMTP'

# Create message
msg = MIMEText(body)
msg['From'] = sender
msg['To'] = receiver
msg['Subject'] = subject

# Connect to SMTP server and send
try:
    server = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com', 587)
    server.starttls()  # Enable encryption
    server.login(sender, 'app_password')
    server.sendmail(sender, receiver, msg.as_string())
    print('Email sent successfully')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error: {e}')
finally:
    server.quit()
examples.explanation: Shows SMTP email sending: connect to SMTP server, use starttls() for secure connection, login with credentials, send message using sendmail(). Port 587 is for SMTP submission with TLS.

examples.title: Simple Telnet Client
examples.description: Demonstrates basic Telnet connection using Python's telnetlib (for educational purposes)
examples.code:
import telnetlib
import getpass

# Connect to Telnet server
tn = telnetlib.Telnet('192.168.1.1', 23)

# Read welcome message
print(tn.read_until(b'login: ')

# Send username
tn.write(b'admin\n')

# Read password prompt
print(tn.read_until(b'Password: '))

# Send password (hidden input)
password = getpass.getpass()
tn.write(password.encode('ascii') + b'\n')

# Execute command
tn.write(b'show interfaces\n')
print(tn.read_until(b'--More--', timeout=2))

# Close connection
tn.write(b'exit\n')
tn.close()
examples.explanation: Demonstrates Telnet client connection: establish TCP connection on port 23, send credentials, execute commands. Note: Telnet is insecure; SSH is recommended for production use.
